items.situation: two students swapping music recommendations because one is bored of the same pop singers

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm so bored of hearing the same pop singers on every playlist. I need something new for studying.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Have you tried any lo-fi hip hop? There's this artist, DJ Marlo, whose beats are perfect for concentrating. No lyrics to distract you.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: No lyrics? That actually sounds ideal. I always end up singing along and losing focus.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. I'll text you the link to his study playlist. It's got about three hours of music.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect, thanks! I'll put it on tonight.

questions.stem: According to the man, why is DJ Marlo's music good for studying?

options.A: It has no lyrics to distract the listener
options.B: It is only ten minutes long
options.D: It is played in the campus library

questions.explanation: 男生明确说 DJ Marlo 的音乐 "No lyrics to distract you"（没有歌词分散注意力），所以 A 正确。B 错：他说播放列表约有三小时，不是十分钟。C 错：女生正是厌倦了流行歌手，而这是 lo-fi hip hop，并非流行歌手。D 错：对话从未提到校园图书馆播放。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.B: Delete all of her pop music
options.C: Listen to the playlist while studying tonight
options.D: Ask the man to sing along with her

questions.explanation: 女生最后说 "I'll put it on tonight"（今晚就听），结合她需要学习用的音乐，可推断她今晚会边学习边听这个播放列表，故 C 正确。A 错：过度推断，她只是接受了男生的推荐，没说要自己做歌单。B 错：过度推断，她只是厌倦流行歌，没说要删掉所有流行音乐。D 错：与对话无关，男生只是发链接，没提到一起唱。

items.situation: two friends discussing a movie one just saw — good twists but too long

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did you see that new mystery film everyone's talking about? I finally watched it last night.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not yet. Was it any good? I heard mixed things.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: The plot twists were incredible — I never saw the ending coming. But honestly, it ran almost three hours. I got restless in the middle.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Three hours? Did the slow part ruin it for you?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Not really. The twists made up for it. I'd still recommend it — just watch it at home so you can take a break.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good call. I'll stream it this weekend then.

questions.stem: What complaint did the woman have about the film?
questions.explanation: 女生说电影 "ran almost three hours"，还在中间坐不住了 ("got restless in the middle")，说明她唯一的不满是太长，故 B 正确。A 错：她说反转 "incredible"（精彩），完全没料到结局，并非可预测。C 错：她说 "never saw the ending coming"，对结局是称赞而非失望。D 错：对话从未提到音效。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do?

options.A: Watch the film in a theater tonight
options.C: Ask the cinema for a refund
options.D: Stream the film at home this weekend

questions.explanation: 男生最后说 "I'll stream it this weekend then"，且女生建议在家看以便中途休息，故 D 正确。A 错：女生明确建议在家看而非影院，男生也没说今晚去影院，属被否决的选项。B 错：过度推断，他并没有说完全不看，反而打算周末看。C 错：与对话无关，没人提到退款。

items.situation: a student returning a too-small sweater at a store counter (service — minority)

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'd like to return this sweater. I bought it last week, but it's a size too small.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: No problem. Do you have your receipt with you?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yes, right here. I was hoping to exchange it for a large instead of a refund.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me check... I'm sorry, we're out of that style in large right now. I can give you a full refund, or order one for you online.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, how long would the online order take?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: About five business days, shipped straight to your home.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's fine, I'm not in a hurry. Let's do the online order.

questions.stem: Why does the woman want to return the sweater?

options.B: It has a hole in it
options.C: It is a size too small

questions.explanation: 女生一开口就说 "it's a size too small"（尺码太小），故 C 正确。A 错：对话从未提到颜色。B 错：没提到破洞，属看似合理但未提及。D 错：她明确说收据 "right here"，收据在手上，并未丢失。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Wait for the larger sweater to arrive by mail
options.B: Come back to the store tomorrow
options.C: Pick out a completely different style

questions.explanation: 女生说 "Let's do the online order"，而男生说网购大码会在约五个工作日内寄到家，故她会在家等大码毛衣寄到，A 正确。B 错：与对话无关，她没说明天回店里。C 错：过度推断，她要的是同款大码，没说换别的款式。D 错：男生提出退款选项，但女生选择了网购下单，退款是被她否决的选项。

items.situation: two students talking about a cat one of them is pet-sitting

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm watching my neighbor's cat this week while she's traveling. It's my first time pet-sitting.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh nice! How's it going so far?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, a little stressful. The cat won't eat, and I'm worried something's wrong. She barely touched her food yesterday.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Cats get picky when their owner's away. Did you keep the same food and feeding spot the owner uses?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I moved the bowl to the kitchen, actually. Maybe that's the problem.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Probably. Put it back where the cat's used to eating. They really don't like change.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That makes sense. I'll move it back tonight.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to find a new apartment
options.B: The woman's plan to adopt a cat
options.C: A cat that has run away
options.D: A problem the woman is having while pet-sitting

questions.explanation: 整段对话围绕女生帮邻居看猫、猫不吃东西的困扰以及如何解决展开，故 D 抓住主旨。A 错：完全不同的校园话题，没提找房子。B 错：把情况说反了，她是在临时照看邻居的猫，并非要领养猫。C 错：猫没有走失，问题是它不吃东西，属另一个话题。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Take the cat to a veterinarian
options.B: Move the food bowl back to its usual spot
options.C: Buy a different brand of cat food
options.D: Ask her neighbor to come home early

questions.explanation: 男生建议把碗放回猫习惯吃饭的地方，女生回应 "I'll move it back tonight"，故 B 正确。A 错：过度推断，两人只谈到换回喂食位置，没提去看兽医。C 错：与对话无关，问题被判断为喂食位置而非食物本身。D 错：过度推断，没人提到让邻居提前回来。

items.situation: two students comparing how they get to campus — bike, bus, or walk

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How do you usually get to campus? I've been taking the bus, but it's always so packed.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I used to bike, but my route got torn up for construction. Now I mostly walk — twenty-five minutes, but at least it's reliable.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Twenty-five minutes isn't bad. The bus is supposed to be faster, but with all the stops it takes just as long.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. And you're stuck if one gets canceled. Walking, I always know when I'll arrive.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Maybe I should try walking too. My apartment's only about a mile away.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely give it a shot. You might actually enjoy the fresh air.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly talking about?

options.A: Different ways of getting to campus
options.B: A construction project near the man's home
options.C: Problems with the campus bus schedule
options.D: The woman's plan to move closer to campus

questions.explanation: 两人比较坐公交、骑车、步行三种到校方式的优劣，故 A 概括了主旨。B 错：施工只是男生弃骑车改步行的一个细节，不是主题，过于狭窄。C 错：公交问题只是其中一部分细节，也过于狭窄。D 错：女生只是说公寓离校约一英里、想试试步行，并没有搬家计划，属曲解情况。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Buy a bicycle for her commute
options.D: Move to an apartment near campus

questions.explanation: 女生说 "Maybe I should try walking too"，并提到公寓只有约一英里，男生也鼓励她试试，故 C 正确。A 错：与对话无关，且男生的骑车路线因施工被毁，两人没谈到买自行车。B 错：过度推断，她只是抱怨公交拥挤，没打算投诉公交公司。D 错：过度推断，她没有搬家的想法，只是想换出行方式。
